summary: End-to-end backend testing of the new 'Trust this device for 30 days' addition to the 2FA flow on both mobile (/api/mobile/auth/login + /api/mobile/auth/2fa/verify) and web cookie (/api/auth/login + /api/auth/2fa/verify) paths. 15/15 new trusted_devices tests pass and 19/19 pre-existing 2FA tests continue to pass after the addition (34/34 total). All scenarios in the review request were exercised: untrusted/known/different/missing/short fingerprints, trust+session issuance, re-trust idempotency, list/delete/revoke-all device management, expired-trust fail-safe, trust_device=true silent no-op without fingerprint, cross-user isolation with same fingerprint string, and web /auth/2fa/verify sensitive-field stripping (totp_secret, totp_pending_secret, recovery_codes, password_hash).

minor.issue: Each trusted-device lookup loops through every record for the user and bcrypt-checks each one (rounds=8). For users with many devices this is O(n) * bcrypt-time. Bounded by 30-day TTL and small per-user device counts, so non-blocking. Could index lookups with a non-secret prefix hint if scale ever justifies it.

Does not bump last_used_at on a trust HIT (only on trust_device write). The /trusted-devices list therefore shows last_used_at = created_at until the user re-runs the 2FA challenge again. Minor UX gap, not a security issue.

critical_code_review_comments: Trusted-device write/read are bcrypt-hashed (rounds=8) and keyed on (user_id, fingerprint_hash) — DB compromise alone cannot grant device trust to an attacker. Good defence in depth.

Fingerprints shorter than 8 chars are silently treated as untrusted both on read AND on write (is_device_trusted + trust_device early-return). This is a sensible defence against trivially-collidable fingerprints; verified in test.
trust_device updates the existing record's expiry rather than inserting duplicates (verified — re-trust after manual expiry yields exactly 1 doc). Good.

Web /api/auth/2fa/verify explicitly strips totp_secret, totp_pending_secret, recovery_codes, and password_hash before returning the user document. Mobile /mobile/auth/2fa/verify shapes via _to_mobile_user which already excludes secrets — consistent.
/api/account/me/2fa/trusted-devices/{device_id} DELETE is correctly scoped to {user_id, device_id} pair, so user A cannot revoke user B's device by guessing device_id (404). Verified.

TTL index on trusted_devices.expires_at_ts is created in ensure_indexes() so expired grants self-purge — verified that an expired record (manually backdated) is treated as untrusted by is_device_trusted even before the TTL sweep fires.
Suggestion (non-blocking): set last_used_at on each successful trust HIT so the management UI reflects real recency. Currently only re-trust updates it.

Suggestion (non-blocking): the global error envelope wraps HTTPException.detail into {ok,false,code,message} — same observation as iteration 1; clients expecting FastAPI's default {detail} shape will see message instead. Consistent across the app.
test_credentials: john@atlas.dev / dev123 and admin@atlas.dev / admin123 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Tests reset both users' 2FA state and trusted_devices via direct MongoDB writes before and after each test using the `enrolled` fixture.
seed_data_creation: None — relied on existing seed users john@atlas.dev and admin@atlas.dev. The `enrolled` fixture enables 2FA for john on every test and tears it down, plus purges trusted_devices. The cross-user isolation test transiently enables 2FA on admin and resets it after.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run: `cd /app && REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://mobile-expo-app-1.preview.emergentagent.com MONGO_URL=mongodb://localhost:27017 DB_NAME=test_database /root/.venv/bin/python -m pytest backend/tests/test_trusted_devices.py backend/tests/test_two_factor.py -v`. Each test is order-independent — the `enrolled` fixture clears both `users` (2FA state) and `trusted_devices` rows for john before+after. Cross-user test (TestCrossUserIsolation, TestTrustedDevicesManagement::test_revoke_all_clears_user_only) also touches admin@atlas.dev and resets it in a `finally` block. Frontend (Expo + web SPA) was skipped per main agent instruction.
rca of the issue: N/A — no functional bugs found. Backend correctly: (1) issues challenge for untrusted/missing/short fingerprints, (2) short-circuits with valid trust both on mobile and web login paths, (3) re-trust updates expiry rather than duplicating, (4) DELETE and revoke-all are user-scoped (admin's trust is preserved when john revokes-all), (5) expired records (forced via mongo) are not treated as trusted, (6) web /auth/2fa/verify strips all four sensitive fields. All 15 trusted_devices tests + 19 pre-existing 2FA tests green.
